title: oxalis cathara
intro: Oxalis cathara, also known as the wood sorrel or bitter sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and fungal infections. The health benefits of oxalis cathara include its ability to act as an antibacterial and antifungal agent, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Oxalis cathara is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in salves and creams to treat skin conditions and wounds.
